purpose: Research-backed SEE+CE synthesis of which sandbox languages/libraries should be routed to which MetaBlooms work classes.

research_sources.topic: JSON Schema for declarative JSON validation

ce_synthesis.main_failure_pattern: LLM used reasoning or fragile text patching where a structural tool should have owned the task.
ce_synthesis.correct_operating_model: Classify task -> route to authoritative tool -> execute -> verify artifact -> record receipt.
ce_synthesis.highest_leverage_shift: Use AST/schema/Git/zip/hash/browser/PDF/document libraries as task-specific authorities rather than treating Python snippets as generic glue.

routing_matrix.secondary_tool: Python ast for structural inspection; safe_code_patch_helpers_v1.py for deterministic line-aware edits
routing_matrix.required_gate: parse source with LibCST, transform, re-render, re-parse, verify marker/behavior, then git diff

routing_matrix.secondary_tool: Node.js for JS syntax/testing; Playwright when browser behavior matters
routing_matrix.avoid: regex HTML parsing for structural edits

routing_matrix.secondary_tool: Node.js test runner for pure JS logic
routing_matrix.avoid: assuming HTML works because file exists

routing_matrix.primary_tool: pdfplumber for layout/tables; pypdf for text/metadata
routing_matrix.secondary_tool: web.run screenshot for web PDFs when visual inspection needed
document skill rules when producing final docx artifacts
artifact_handoff when user asks for presentations
routing_matrix.avoid: HTML/images posing as editable slide deck

routing_matrix.primary_tool: openpyxl for XLSX; pandas/csv for CSV audit

new_governance_requirements: Every stage must declare work_class and selected_tool before execution.
If selected_tool is unavailable, probe and record fallback before proceeding.
Code patch tasks must use AST or safe line-aware helper with post-write verification.
HTML structural edits must use parser-based tools where possible.
Schema/contract claims must be checked with jsonschema when schema exists.
Browser behavior claims require browser/runtime test when available.
Artifact export claims require zip/hash/manifest verification.
Research-triggered tasks require web.run evidence and citations.
For Python code transformations where formatting/comments must be preserved, prefer vendored LibCST over regex/string patching.
Before Python code patching/codemod work, run ensure_libcst_available_v1.py; install/vendor LibCST if missing; then use LibCST as primary structural patch tool.
